Karim Ahmed is a Sr. Network Engineer with contact information including email, phone number, GitHub, and LinkedIn profiles, as well as a personal website.
Karim Ahmed obtained a M.Sc. in Network Administration from AISECT University in Bhopal, India, from 2013 to 2015, and a B.Com. from Jiwaji University, Gwalior, India, from 2010 to 2013.

From January 2022 to the present, Karim Ahmed worked as a Sr. Network Engineer at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd., where he led router, switch, and server configurations, managed WiFi access points, implemented backups, and administered Windows Servers.

From January 2020 to December 2021, Karim Ahmed was an IT Executive at Asha MMPC, configuring servers, implementing backups, administering Windows Servers, and troubleshooting LAN & WAN infrastructure.

From January 2018 to December 2019, Karim Ahmed served as a Network Engineer at Interface Techno System, leading network infrastructure implementation, maintaining backups, administering Windows and Linux servers, and troubleshooting LAN infrastructure.

From January 2016 to December 2017, the individual supported network implementation and configuration projects for clients like Teva API India Pvt Ltd, maintained backup schedules using Symantec Backup Exec 12D, and restored files. They also upgraded Cisco router configurations and administered Windows and Linux servers.

The individual troubleshot LAN infrastructure, configured Cisco routers and switches, managed Check Point Endpoint Security, conducted health checks on servers, used CA Uni-center Service Desk Manager, managed Windows XP networks, installed printers and software, and managed user accounts and E-Tokens for over 350 desktops and laptops.
The individual generated monthly and weekly reports for servers, MIS, vendor, and inventory management.

From January 2014 to December 2015, the individual managed servers and implemented backup strategies for Nava Bharat Press (P) Ltd, provided helpdesk support, monitored SLA compliance, hosted client meetings, and performed system management and troubleshooting.

The individual spearheaded the configuration of WiFi APs for railway stations in Maharashtra, including planning, cabling, installation, commissioning, and handover, for Railwire/Railtel Corporation of India Ltd.
The individual executed server configurations for DPMCU, Milknet, EMS, and QMS systems in Bali, Pali, and Rajasthan, India, for Asha MMPC.
The individual implemented and configured network infrastructure, including routers, switches, and servers, for The Scindia School in Fort, Gwalior, India.
The individual implemented network implementation and configuration for M/s Teva API India Pvt Ltd in Bhind.
The individual managed comprehensive network implementation and configuration projects in India, ensuring seamless network operations and connectivity.
The individual holds a Tally ERP9 Certification from Tally Academy, obtained in 2013, covering both manual and computerized accounting.
The individual possesses skills in various operating systems including Windows (7, 10, XP), Linux, Ubuntu, RHEL 6, and Windows Server versions (2000, 2003, 2008 R2, 2012 R2, 2016).

The individual is skilled in networking technologies such as Cisco Routing & Switching, InterVLAN Routing, RIPv2, RIPng, OSPF, EIGRP, Static Routing, ACL, VTP, VLAN, EtherChannel, HSRP, STP, IPv6, LAN/WAN Troubleshooting, Structured Cabling, Cisco Firewall, Fortinet Firewall, DHCP, and DNS.
The individual has expertise in server technologies including Active Directory (AD, RODC), Print Server, SCCM, WDS, DHCP, Group Policies, DNS Server, RIS Server, TFTP, and User/Computer Policies.
The individual is proficient in virtualization and backup technologies such as VMWare ESXi, VMware Workstation, Oracle VirtualBox, GNS3, Symantec Backup EXEC 12D, and Server 2016 Backup Tools.
The individual is familiar with networking devices including Cisco Routers (1800s-7200s), Cisco Switches (2900s, 3700s, 4850s), HP ProLiant Servers, Dell/Lenovo Servers, Fortinet Firewall, and Modem.

The individual utilizes various management and tools such as Helpdesk, SLA Monitoring, Incident Management, CA Unicenter Service Desk Manager, Check Point Endpoint Security MI Management Console, Microsoft Outlook, Tally ERP9, MIS Reporting, Vendor/Inventory Management, and Hardware Troubleshooting.

Linux is a quietly powerful presence, a foundational element often unseen but constantly at work. Not flashy, not demanding of attention, it’s a robust and versatile operating system, a workhorse built for stability and control. It’s the kind of system a skilled hand – like Karim Ahmed’s – instinctively turns to when building and maintaining complex networks. He understands its depth, its capacity to be molded to specific needs, whether administering servers at Interface Techno System or ensuring smooth operations for clients like Teva API India. 

Linux isn’t a solitary entity, though. It thrives under careful management, responding to a practiced touch. Karim Ahmed, for instance, didn’t just *use* Linux; he *managed* it, a testament to its complexity and the expertise required to unlock its full potential. It’s a system that demands respect, rewarding diligent administration with unwavering performance. While often working alongside Windows, Linux provides a crucial backbone, handling server duties and underpinning critical infrastructure. It’s a silent partner, a reliable foundation upon which more visible systems operate, a testament to its enduring utility in the digital landscape.

Nestled in the historic fort of Gwalior, India, The Scindia School stands as a beacon of tradition and modern learning. More than just an institution, it’s a carefully constructed environment – a network, in a very real sense – brought to life through meticulous planning and execution. In recent years, this infrastructure underwent a significant evolution, guided by the expertise of individuals like Karim Ahmed. He arrived as a digital architect, responsible for weaving together the complex threads of routers, switches, and servers that now underpin the school’s operations. His work ensured a robust and reliable network, safeguarding vital data through diligent backups and maintaining seamless connectivity across the campus. The school, steeped in history within the walls of Gwalior Fort, now benefits from a contemporary digital backbone, a testament to the blend of heritage and progress that defines its character. It’s a place where ancient stones meet modern technology, all within the heart of India.

The Cisco Router stands as a stalwart of network infrastructure, a silent architect of data’s journey. Not a flashy component, but a foundational one – a workhorse relied upon by engineers like Karim Ahmed across diverse environments, from the pharmaceutical sector at Teva API India to the broader network landscapes of Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd. and Asha MMPC. It’s a device frequently found at the crossroads of Local Area Networks (LAN) and Wide Area Networks (WAN), directing traffic with quiet efficiency. 

While often unseen by the end-user, its configuration is paramount. Karim Ahmed, and others like him, have dedicated portions of their careers to its meticulous setup and ongoing maintenance. It’s a machine that demands attention to detail, requiring periodic upgrades – Ahmed himself has overseen these improvements – and, crucially, consistent backups. These backups, often managed with tools like Symantec Backup Exec, are its safety net, ensuring network resilience against unforeseen failures. 

The Cisco Router isn’t merely a piece of hardware; it’s a node in a complex web, a critical link in the chain that connects businesses and individuals. It’s a testament to reliable, if understated, technology, consistently serving as a cornerstone of modern connectivity and a frequent focus of skilled network professionals.

India is a land steeped in history and burgeoning with modern ambition, a vast and complex nation where ancient traditions meet rapid technological advancement. It’s a place where education, from commerce degrees at institutions like Jiwaji University in Gwalior to specialized network administration programs at AISECT University in Bhopal, fuels a growing workforce. This workforce, exemplified by individuals like Karim Ahmed, is instrumental in building and maintaining the nation’s increasingly sophisticated infrastructure. 

Ahmed’s work – configuring servers for systems like DPMCU and Milknet across Rajasthan, Pali, and Bali, and establishing robust network foundations for esteemed institutions like The Scindia School in Gwalior – speaks to India’s expanding digital landscape. The country isn’t merely adopting technology; it’s actively shaping it, ensuring connectivity and seamless operations across diverse regions. 

From the historic city of Gwalior to the developing hub of Bhopal, India is a network of interconnected locations, each contributing to the nation’s overall progress. The Scindia School, nestled within its borders, represents a commitment to education and a future built on knowledge and innovation. India is a nation defined by its geographical breadth, its dedication to learning, and its relentless pursuit of a connected, modern future. It’s a place where skilled professionals are actively laying the groundwork for continued growth and global relevance.

Symantec Backup Exec 12D is a seasoned workhorse in the realm of data protection, a reliable if somewhat dated solution for safeguarding critical information. It’s a tool favored by system administrators needing to establish and maintain consistent backup schedules – a task it performs with dependable, if not flashy, efficiency. While newer technologies have emerged, Backup Exec 12D remains a known quantity, capable of restoring files when disaster strikes or data is lost. It’s particularly valued by those comfortable navigating established systems, and often found alongside virtualization platforms like VMware ESXi and Oracle VirtualBox, demonstrating its adaptability to diverse IT environments. Though often paired with Windows Server 2016’s native backup tools, Backup Exec 12D offers a dedicated, focused approach to backup and recovery, proving its worth in supporting network infrastructure for organizations like Teva API India Pvt Ltd. It’s a testament to its enduring functionality that it continues to be a proficient component in a broader skillset encompassing server administration and network configuration.

DPMCU is a system – a critical, though quietly essential, component within the operational landscape of Asha MMPC. It exists as a configured entity, brought into being through the skilled hands of IT Executive Karim Ahmed. While its precise function isn’t outwardly declared, its presence is felt across Asha MMPC’s operations in Bali, Pali, and Rajasthan, India. It’s a foundational layer, a digital backbone supporting the larger systems of Milknet, EMS, and QMS. DPMCU isn’t a flashy innovation, but a reliably functioning piece of infrastructure, meticulously set up and maintained – a silent workhorse enabling the flow of data and the continuation of business. Its story is one of quiet competence, defined by its configuration and its dependence on skilled technical expertise. It *is* configured, and that configuration is its defining characteristic, inextricably linked to the work of Karim Ahmed.

Windows Servers are a foundational element within the digital landscapes maintained by seasoned IT professionals like Karim Ahmed. They aren’t merely machines; they are central hubs, consistently entrusted with the critical task of data storage, security, and application delivery. Across roles at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd., Asha MMPC, and Interface Techno System – and even during client projects for companies like Teva API India Pvt Ltd – Ahmed’s expertise has consistently revolved around their administration. 

These servers represent a stable, yet adaptable, backbone, capable of operating alongside Linux counterparts, demonstrating a versatility valued in diverse network environments. Their upkeep demands meticulous attention to detail, encompassing configuration, consistent backups (often utilizing tools like Symantec Backup Exec 12D), and proactive troubleshooting of both local area (LAN) and wide area (WAN) networks. They are the silent workhorses supporting network infrastructure, requiring skilled hands to ensure seamless operation and data integrity – a responsibility consistently held by individuals like Ahmed, who have demonstrably maintained and optimized their performance over years of dedicated service. They are, in essence, the reliable core upon which modern network functionality depends.

EMS is a system quietly underpinning Asha MMPC’s operations, a digital backbone configured and maintained by IT Executive Karim Ahmed between 2020 and 2021. It exists as one among a quartet of crucial systems – DPMCU, Milknet, and QMS – all brought online and tailored by Ahmed across Asha MMPC’s sites in Bali, Pali, and Rajasthan, India. While its precise function remains veiled within the larger network, EMS clearly relies on careful server configuration and ongoing administration. It’s a silent workhorse, brought into being by a specific technical expertise, and essential to the smooth running of Asha MMPC’s wider infrastructure. Its existence is defined by its connection to Ahmed’s work, a testament to his skill in building and maintaining the unseen systems that keep things moving.

These servers aren’t just boxes humming in a data center; they are the quiet backbone of operations, meticulously shaped and maintained by skilled hands. They’ve known the focused attention of Karim Ahmed, a network engineer who treats them as critical components – and rightly so. At Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd. since 2022, Ahmed doesn’t simply *manage* these servers; he orchestrates their configurations, ensuring routers, switches, and the servers themselves work in seamless harmony. He’s a guardian of their data, implementing robust backup strategies and diligently monitoring their performance, translating that information into clear monthly and weekly reports. 

Before Cloudatix, at Nava Bharat Press (P) Ltd, these servers were entrusted to Ahmed’s care from 2014 to 2015. There, they weren’t just technical assets, but vital to meeting service level agreements and supporting client interactions. He wasn’t just a troubleshooter, but a proactive system manager, ensuring smooth operation and swift resolution of any issue. Even earlier, at The Scindia School, Ahmed brought these servers to life, configuring the entire network infrastructure around them – a foundational role that speaks to his deep understanding of their importance. 

These servers are more than just hardware; they are repositories of information, facilitators of communication, and ultimately, extensions of the individuals who depend on them. They reflect the precision and dedication of those who configure, monitor, and protect them, standing as silent witnesses to the flow of data and the success of the organizations they serve.

Karim Ahmed is a seasoned Sr. Network Engineer, a meticulous architect of digital infrastructure with a solid academic foundation built upon a B.Com. from Jiwaji University (2010-2013) and a M.Sc. in Network Administration from AISECT University (2013-2015). He’s a practical problem-solver, equally comfortable navigating the intricacies of Windows and Linux servers, and adept at ensuring seamless network connectivity. 

Currently at Cloudatix Network Pvt. Ltd. since January 2022, Karim leads the configuration of routers, switches, and servers, maintaining the vital arteries of their network. He’s responsible for robust backup systems – including Cisco router backups – and the smooth operation of WiFi access points. Prior to this, as an IT Executive at Asha MMPC (2020-2021), he honed his server configuration skills, specifically with DPMCU, Milknet, EMS, and QMS servers, and expertly troubleshooted LAN & WAN issues. His career began taking shape at Interface Techno System (2018-2019) as a Network Engineer, where he spearheaded network infrastructure implementation, notably for The Scindia School, and maintained critical backups. 

Karim isn’t simply a technician; he’s a builder, a maintainer, and a troubleshooter, consistently ensuring systems run efficiently and securely. He maintains an active online presence through his website (karimahmed.com), LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/karim-ahmed), and GitHub (github.com/karim-ahmed), and can be reached at karim.ahmed@email.com or +20 101 234 5678.

MILKNET is a critical system within Asha MMPC’s infrastructure, quietly underpinning operations across Bali, Pali, and Rajasthan, India. It isn’t a flashy face of the company, but a foundational element – a configured environment, meticulously set up and maintained by IT Executive Karim Ahmed between 2020 and 2021. While its precise function remains veiled within the larger Asha MMPC network, MILKNET exists as a configured entity, a digital space brought into being by Ahmed’s expertise. It stands alongside DPMCU, EMS, and QMS as one of the core systems he brought online, suggesting a role in data management or operational control. MILKNET doesn’t *do* so much as *enable* – a silent workhorse, reliant on consistent administration and a stable network connection, and fundamentally shaped by the hands of its initial architect. Its existence is defined by its configuration, and its continued function by the legacy of that initial setup.
